Class WorkBatch.WorkItem

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.WorkBatch.WorkItem
Enclosing class:
WorkBatch
public static class WorkBatch.WorkItem extends Object
A compact, reusable representation of a range of combinations that share a common prefix.

This is the fundamental unit of work within a WorkBatch. Instead of storing millions of complete combinations, a WorkItem describes a set of combinations by storing a shared prefix and a WorkBatch.Parity value that determines the array of possible final clicks. The monkey can then iterate through the final clicks efficiently.

Object Lifecycle

WorkItem instances are pre-allocated within a WorkBatch and are reused to eliminate GC pressure. A generator calls set(short[], Parity, int) to populate a recycled item, and clear() is called when the WorkBatch itself is recycled.

Since:
2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
See Also:
  • WorkBatch.addWork(short[], short, boolean)
Performance Characteristics:
Accessors are O(1). No performance-critical methods.
Threading Model:
Not thread-safe. Instances are owned and operated on by a single thread at a time.
Memory Management:
The object contains references to a prefix array and a final clicks array but does not own the latter. Minimal, fixed overhead per instance.

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private WorkBatch.Parity
    finalClickParity
    The WorkBatch.Parity of the prefix, which determines whether to use StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig.ODD_CLICK_INDICES or StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig.EVEN_CLICK_INDICES for the final click.
    private short[]
    prefix
    The shared prefix of the combinations.
    private int
    start
    The starting index within the final clicks array (retrieved via getFinalClicks()) from which the monkey should begin testing.

  • Field Details

    • prefix

      private short[] prefix
      The shared prefix of the combinations. The contents of this array are copied from the input provided by set(short[], Parity, int), ensuring that external modifications do not affect this work item.

      For better performance, this field could be made final.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • getPrefix()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) iteration, O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 2 * (numClicks - 1) bytes for the short[] array.

    • finalClickParity

      private WorkBatch.Parity finalClickParity
      The WorkBatch.Parity of the prefix, which determines whether to use StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig.ODD_CLICK_INDICES or StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig.EVEN_CLICK_INDICES for the final click. This replaces the direct short[] reference to save memory.
      Since:
      2025.12 - Parity Enum Refactor
      See Also:
      • getFinalClicks()
      • WorkBatch.Parity
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal footprint for an enum reference.

    • start

      private int start
      The starting index within the final clicks array (retrieved via getFinalClicks()) from which the monkey should begin testing. This allows a generator to create a WorkItem that represents a sub-range of the final clicks.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • getStart()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the primitive int.

  • Constructor Details

    • WorkItem

      WorkItem()
      Constructs a new WorkItem, pre-allocating its internal prefix array.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if numClicks has not been set.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      See Also:
      • WorkBatch.getNumClicks()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(numClicks - 1) allocation for the prefix array.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates memory for the prefix array.

  • Method Details

    • set

      void set(short[] prefix, WorkBatch.Parity finalClickParity, int start)
      Initializes or re-initializes the WorkItem with its data.

      This method is internally called by WorkBatch.addWork(short[], short, boolean) to fill a recycled WorkItem with the necessary data. The provided prefix is copied into the internal array to prevent external modifications from affecting this item.

      Parameters:
      prefix - The common prefix for this range of combinations.
      finalClickParity - The WorkBatch.Parity indicating which set of final clicks to use.
      start - The starting index in the finalClicks array.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) for array copy; O(1) for field assignments.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; reuses internal arrays.

    • clear

      void clear()
      Resets the WorkItem to a clean state, ready for reuse.

      This is called when the parent WorkBatch is cleared. It clears references to external arrays but does not null out the internal prefix array, allowing it to be recycled without a new allocation.

      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for field assignments.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getPrefix

      public short[] getPrefix()
      Returns the shared combination prefix for this work item.
      Returns:
      The prefix array.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; returns reference to existing array.

    • getPrefixLength

      public int getPrefixLength()
      Returns the length of the shared combination prefix.
      Returns:
      The prefix length, or -1 if not set.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getFinalClicks

      public short[] getFinalClicks()
      Returns the array of possible final clicks for this work range by retrieving it from the finalClickParity enum.
      Returns:
      The array of final clicks, or null if parity is not set.
      Since:
      2025.12 - Parity Enum Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; returns reference to existing array.

    • getStart

      public int getStart()
      Returns the starting index within the final clicks array} from which a monkey should begin processing.
      Returns:
      The start index.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a String representation of the WorkItem, useful for debugging.

      The format is WorkItem{prefix=[...], prefixLength=..., finalClickParity=...}. The final clicks portion only includes the elements from the start index to the end of the array.

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A String representation of the object.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength + finalClicks.length).
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new StringBuilder and a new String, with indirect allocations from Arrays.copyOfRange(short[], int, int) and Arrays.toString(short[]).

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Compares this WorkItem to another object for equality.

      Two WorkItems are considered equal if their prefix, finalClickParity, and start fields are all equal.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - The Object to compare with.
      Returns:
      true if the objects are equal, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) in the worst case due to array comparisons.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for the object.

      The hash code is calculated based on the contents of the prefix array, the finalClickParity enum, as well as the start index.

      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      A hash code value for this object.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) due to array hash code computations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
